Our Story

Preserving Heritage,
Building Community

The Arizona Telugu Association (AZTA) is a dedicated community organization that seeks to preserve and promote the rich Telugu culture and heritage within the state of Arizona. Serving the growing Telugu-speaking population, AZTA provides a platform for individuals and families to celebrate traditions, engage in cultural activities, and strengthen their connection to their roots.

🎯 Our Mission

Fostering a sense of community among Telugu-speaking residents through cultural, educational, and social events — keeping Telugu language, traditions, and values alive for future generations.

🌿 Our Vision

Promoting unity among all Indian communities in Arizona while celebrating the beauty of Telugu culture and traditions, ensuring they thrive for generations to come.

🎭 Festivals

We organize Ugadi, Sankranti, Diwali and more — showcasing vibrant performances, traditional food, music, dance, and festive activities for all ages.

🤝 Community

Supporting charity initiatives and collaborating with other Indian associations to promote diversity, cultural exchange, and togetherness across Arizona.

Join Our Community →
Events & Festivals

Celebrate With Us

⭐ Featured
12APR

Ugadi 2025 — Telugu New Year Celebration

📅 April 12, 2025📍 Chandler Center for the Arts⏰ 5:00 PM

Ring in the Telugu New Year with traditional rituals, cultural performances, Ugadi pachadi, music & dance. A family celebration not to be missed!

Bathukamma 2025

📅 Oct 5, 2025📍 Phoenix, AZ

The vibrant floral festival of Telangana with traditional Bathukamma arrangements, songs, and folk dances celebrating womanhood.

Community Programs

Nurturing Culture
Across Generations

From language classes for the little ones to cultural workshops for elders — AZTA has something for every member of your family.

📖

Telugu Language Classes

Weekly classes for children taught by experienced educators, covering reading, writing, and conversation in Telugu to preserve our mother tongue.

Enroll Now →
💃

Classical Dance & Music

Bharatanatyam, Kuchipudi, Carnatic music classes and more — connecting youth to the richness of Telugu performing arts and cultural identity.

Learn More →
🎭

Cultural Workshops

Kolam drawing, Kalamkari art, traditional cooking, and other hands-on workshops celebrating Telugu craft, food, and culture throughout the year.

Explore →
👦

Youth Leadership

Mentorship programs, essay competitions, and leadership activities helping young Telugu Americans build confidence and community pride.

Get Involved →
🤝

Volunteer Corps

Make a difference by joining our volunteer network — help organize events, mentor youth, or support charitable causes across Arizona.

Volunteer →
🌿

Senior Connect

Special gatherings, health resources, and social activities tailored for our beloved elders — fostering connection, care, and cultural pride.

Join Us →

Community Partners

Membership & Support

Be Part of the Family

Your membership strengthens our community and helps us preserve Telugu culture for generations to come.

Individual
$25/year
  • Access to all AZTA events
  • Monthly community newsletter
  • Voting rights in AZTA elections
  • Member directory access
Most Popular
Family
$50/year
  • All individual benefits for whole family
  • Discounted event registration
  • Youth program enrollment priority
  • Invitation to exclusive member events
  • Sponsor recognition at events
Patron
$200/year
  • All family benefits
  • Name recognition in event programs
  • VIP seating at major events
  • Direct involvement in planning committees

Support Telugu Culture in Arizona

Your generous donations fund cultural programs, youth scholarships, language classes, and community outreach. Every contribution makes a difference.
